refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-006 (Refined): The NGHE solution shall generate and maintain a real-time worksite model representing terrain elevation, static obstacles (rocks, structures, vegetation), dynamic hazards (personnel, other equipment), and designated work zones with sufficient fidelity to support autonomous path planning, collision avoidance, and operator situational awareness.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is vague on fidelity requirements, update frequency, and data sources. Refinement clarifies that the worksite model must be actionable for both planning (pre-execution) and execution (real-time safety). High safety criticality demands explicit hazard representation and dynamic update capability. The model must be consumable by downstream planning and safety functions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Acceptance: (1) Model generated within 5 minutes of site entry; (2) Terrain elevation accuracy ±0.5 m; (3) Obstacle detection range ≥50 m; (4) Dynamic hazard update latency <2 s; (5) Model accessible via standardized interface to planning and safety modules; (6) Coverage of agreed site feature set per worksite safety plan.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain and obstacle data from onboard sensors and external sources.
CR-NGHE-006.2: Fuse multi-source sensor data into a unified worksite model representation.
CR-NGHE-006.3: Detect and classify static obstacles (terrain features, structures, vegetation).
CR-NGHE-006.4: Detect and track dynamic hazards (personnel, moving equipment).
CR-NGHE-006.5: Represent and update designated work zones and safety boundaries.
CR-NGHE-006.6: Publish worksite model to planning and safety functions via standardized interface.
CR-NGHE-006.7: Validate model completeness and accuracy against worksite safety plan requirements.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Onboard LiDAR + camera fusion with local SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) algorithm; model stored in vehicle memory; published via Telemetry interface.
DP-B: Onboard sensor suite (LiDAR, camera, IMU, GNSS) + cloud-based map service; real-time data streamed to cloud; model computed and cached centrally; accessed via Network Link.
DP-C: Pre-loaded high-resolution satellite/survey map + onboard sensor-based dynamic layer (LiDAR for obstacles, camera for hazards); local fusion; published via Telemetry.
DP-D: Tele-operator visual input (camera feed) + onboard proximity sensors; operator-assisted model building via manual annotation; model stored locally and synchronized via Network Link.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Pre-loaded high-resolution satellite/survey map as static terrain baseline + onboard LiDAR and camera for real-time obstacle and dynamic hazard detection; local sensor fusion engine; model published via Telemetry interface to planning and safety modules.

potential_sources: Dependency on pre-mission map availability (external to NGHE solution); mitigation: fallback to onboard SLAM if map unavailable.
Sensor fusion algorithm couples LiDAR and camera calibration; mitigation: factory calibration and periodic validation.
Model fidelity depends on map resolution and sensor range; mitigation: acceptance criteria specify minimum fidelity.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C balances real-time responsiveness (onboard processing) with accuracy (pre-loaded map baseline). Avoids cloud dependency (DP-B) which introduces latency and network risk. Avoids operator burden (DP-D). Requires pre-mission planning but aligns with worksite safety plan requirement.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Pre-mission satellite/survey map is available and current (within 30 days of deployment).
ASSUMPTION: Onboard LiDAR and camera are calibrated and functional.
ASSUMPTION: Network connectivity is available for map download but not required for real-time model updates.
RISK: Map obsolescence if site changes between survey and deployment; mitigation: operator override and manual annotation capability.
RISK: Sensor degradation (mud, dust on optics) reduces detection range; mitigation: sensor health monitoring and alerts.
RISK: Computational load of real-time fusion may exceed onboard processing budget; mitigation: algorithm optimization and hardware specification.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify map update frequency and fallback strategy if pre-loaded map is unavailable or stale. Specify sensor fusion algorithm (e.g., Kalman filter, occupancy grid) and computational resource budget. Define model refresh rate (e.g., 10 Hz for dynamic hazards, 1 Hz for static obstacles).

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Onboard worksite mapping subsystem: LiDAR and camera sensor acquisition, sensor fusion engine, local model storage, and Telemetry publication. Includes sensor drivers, fusion algorithm, and model data structure. Does NOT include pre-loaded map storage (external) or cloud processing (out of scope for Vehicle module).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle publishes worksite model (terrain grid, obstacle list, dynamic hazard positions, work zone boundaries) to planning and safety modules. Format: structured data (e.g., JSON or protobuf). Frequency: 10 Hz for dynamic hazards, 1 Hz for static model updates.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives mission tasking and worksite safety plan parameters (e.g., work zone boundaries, obstacle avoidance thresholds, hazard classification rules) to configure model generation and validation.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle publishes safety alerts if model detects anomalies (e.g., unexpected obstacle, personnel in exclusion zone, sensor failure) to Safety Services module.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system; worksite mapping is a subsystem function. Assigning to NGHE Solution would diffuse the mapping responsibility across multiple child modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) without clear ownership. Not recommended as primary IM boundary.

Vehicle is the natural owner of onboard sensor acquisition, fusion, and local model storage. Sensors (LiDAR, camera) are vehicle-mounted. Fusion algorithm runs on vehicle compute. Model is published from vehicle to other modules. High containment, minimal diffusion. RECOMMENDED.

Teleoperation module handles remote operator interface and control. Worksite mapping is not a teleoperation function; it is a vehicle autonomy function. Assigning here would diffuse mapping responsibility away from the vehicle where sensors reside. Not recommended.

Safety Services consumes the worksite model to validate safety constraints and generate alerts. It does not generate the model. Assigning mapping to Safety Services would create a circular dependency and diffuse sensor management responsibility. Not recommended.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Vehicle module owns onboard sensor acquisition, fusion, and model generation. The worksite model is a Vehicle-internal state that is published externally via Telemetry. This aligns with the principle that a module owns the resources (sensors, compute) and algorithms required to produce its outputs.
containment_risks: RISK: If Vehicle module becomes too large (sensor drivers + fusion + model storage + other autonomy functions), consider refactoring into sub-modules (e.g., Perception, Localization, Planning). Mitigation: Define clear sub-module boundaries and interfaces within Vehicle.

RISK: Model publication via Telemetry may create high-bandwidth demand if model is large or update frequency is high. Mitigation: Compress model representation and tune update frequency based on downstream consumer requirements.
RISK: Vehicle module depends on pre-loaded map (external data source). If map is unavailable, fallback to onboard SLAM must be implemented. Mitigation: Specify fallback algorithm and test coverage.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the worksite model data structure (e.g., occupancy grid, point cloud, feature list) and serialization format. Specify sensor fusion algorithm and computational resource budget. Define Telemetry message schema and publication frequency. Clarify ownership of map download and validation (likely a pre-mission planning function, not Vehicle runtime responsibility).

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (pre-loaded map + onboard sensor fusion) directly addresses the refined FR requirement to generate a real-time worksite model with terrain, obstacles, and work zones. DP-C is feasible, aligns with high safety criticality (onboard processing, no cloud dependency), and supports the acceptance criteria (model available to planning/execution functions). No decoupling issues identified.

diffusion_risks: Vehicle module may accumulate multiple autonomy functions (perception, localization, planning, control). Mitigation: Define clear sub-module boundaries and interfaces.
Telemetry interface may become a bottleneck if model publication frequency or size exceeds network capacity. Mitigation: Optimize model representation and tune publication frequency.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module has high containment (0.95) for worksite mapping. Onboard sensors, fusion algorithm, and model storage are naturally contained within Vehicle. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) are well-defined and minimal. No diffusion risk identified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via simulation (sensor simulation, map injection) and field testing (sensor accuracy, fusion latency, model completeness). Acceptance criteria are measurable (terrain accuracy, obstacle detection range, update latency, interface availability).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: Vehicle module owns worksite mapping generation; Safety Services and planning modules consume the model via Telemetry. Ownership boundaries are explicit.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to specify sensor fusion algorithm (e.g., Kalman filter, occupancy grid, feature-based SLAM) and computational resource budget.
Define worksite model data structure and Telemetry message schema (e.g., JSON, protobuf, ROS message).
Specify fallback strategy if pre-loaded map is unavailable (e.g., onboard SLAM, operator manual annotation).
Define model validation criteria and acceptance thresholds (e.g., terrain accuracy ±0.5 m, obstacle detection range ≥50 m).
Clarify pre-mission planning responsibilities (map acquisition, validation, download to vehicle) vs. runtime responsibilities (model generation, publication).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-006 is not atomic; it comprises multiple sub-functions (sensor acquisition, data fusion, obstacle detection, dynamic hazard tracking, work zone representation, model publication, validation). Each sub-FR requires detailed DP and IM assignment. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to allocate design responsibility and verify feasibility.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain and obstacle data from onboard sensors and external sources.
CR-NGHE-006.2: Fuse multi-source sensor data into a unified worksite model representation.
CR-NGHE-006.3: Detect and classify static obstacles (terrain features, structures, vegetation).
CR-NGHE-006.4: Detect and track dynamic hazards (personnel, moving equipment).
CR-NGHE-006.5: Represent and update designated work zones and safety boundaries.
CR-NGHE-006.6: Publish worksite model to planning and safety functions via standardized interface.
CR-NGHE-006.7: Validate model completeness and accuracy against worksite safety plan requirements.

assumptions: Pre-mission satellite/survey map is available and current (within 30 days of deployment).
Onboard LiDAR and camera are calibrated and functional.
Network connectivity is available for map download but not required for real-time model updates.
Worksite safety plan specifies agreed set of site features and safety boundaries.
Downstream planning and safety modules can consume worksite model via Telemetry interface.
Computational resources on Vehicle are sufficient for real-time sensor fusion (10 Hz dynamic, 1 Hz static).
open_risks: Map obsolescence if site changes between survey and deployment; mitigation: operator override and manual annotation capability.
Sensor degradation (mud, dust on optics) reduces detection range; mitigation: sensor health monitoring and alerts.
Computational load of real-time fusion may exceed onboard processing budget; mitigation: algorithm optimization and hardware specification.
High-bandwidth Telemetry publication may exceed network capacity; mitigation: model compression and frequency tuning.
Fallback to onboard SLAM if pre-loaded map unavailable; mitigation: implement and test SLAM algorithm.
evidence_needed: Sensor specifications (LiDAR range, resolution, update rate; camera resolution, FOV).
Sensor fusion algorithm design and computational complexity analysis.
Worksite model data structure and serialization format specification.
Telemetry interface bandwidth and latency requirements.
Pre-mission map acquisition and validation process.
Fallback SLAM algorithm design and performance characterization.
Field test results: terrain accuracy, obstacle detection range, dynamic hazard tracking latency, model publication frequency.
